hard.next_step: 6.29.26 KK - no update 6.22.26 KK - invited to horizon 6.16.26 KK - fu with Jose on updates with hiring 5.26.26 KK - same - holding pattern until hiring 5.7.26 KK. -rassinin had headcount changes, jose is looking to hire and replace jesus who left. once he hires the project will restart and they would like us onsite for the demonstration 5.4.26 KK - FU on scheduling - asked for onsite 4.14.26 KK - provided availability for next week. If no response, going to push for in person 4.1.26 KK -received workflows - aligning calendars for formal demo week of 13 3.30.26 KK - FU with Jose on availability and his use cases and criteria
hard.ais_why:
• Zycus has logged AI importance as 'Neutral', the explicit middle-of-the-road indicator. The agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary and AI-RFP rating fields are all empty, so there is no captured buyer commentary on Merlin, agentic, GenAI or autonomous negotiation use cases anywhere in Salesforce.
• On the Feb 24 call (32 minutes) Jesus Ochoa kept the discussion on success criteria, ERP feature implementation timing and document translation. Jesus committed to send written success criteria, and the team agreed to draft a Zycus version of the prioritized pilot features. Every action item is classical S2P scoping language, with no AI references.
• The earlier Jan 14 and May 7 calls were similarly clean of AI signal. Combined with a 'Neutral' AI rating and explicit ERP-integration scoping conversations, the buyer is clearly anchored on traditional S2P process automation. There is nothing in the record to push this above the Resistant band.

north_star_verdict.headline: A single-facility S2P pilot has been frozen since 7 May, when IT lead Jesus Ochoa resigned and Jose Antonio Perez placed the project on hold pending a successor; there is no restart date, no delivered success criteria and no demo, so momentum is entirely gated on Rassini's hiring.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 9 Jan 2026; 148 days to close (1 Dec 2026). In Qualified since creation with a brief move to Formal Evaluation on 26 Feb that reverted to Qualified on 11 Jun, so no durable stage progression. Last substantive buyer call 7 May 2026 (hold call); last buyer touch an incoming email 24 Jun declining a near-term Horizon commitment. Roughly eight weeks frozen with no evaluation activity; the Dec close needs a full compressed cycle (hire, onsite demo, scoping, pricing, budget sign-off, contract) into under five months once hiring completes.
evidence: Project explicitly placed on hold on the 7 May call after IT lead Jesus Ochoa resigned: Perez said 'we will put on hold this project until we find the successor of Jesus'.
No forward evaluation motion since 7 May; Next Step reads 'no update' on 29 Jun and 'holding pattern until hiring' on 26 May.
Success criteria committed by Perez on 24 Feb and volume/pricing inputs committed 14 Jan were never delivered, leaving the demo and pricing paths blocked.
Perez's 24 Jun reply ('too early for me to confirm... when the date gets closer I will let you know') is a status touch, not project progress.
Deal is single-threaded on Perez; the budget-owning finance manager and the plant-director boss have never joined a call, so there is no path to advance while hiring is unresolved.

metrics.narrative: Perez articulated sharp operational pain but never quantified it in money. On the 24 Feb discovery call he described approval cycles of 7 to 10 days for every purchase 'even if we are buying a single pen', and said that left unattended 'it would take a month or more to be approved', with users, finance and his boss all unhappy. He framed the goal as 'bringing transparency to the process' and cutting cycle time across a decentralised base of roughly 10 to 15 people buying and 10 approving with no central purchasing department. The financial case (savings target, headcount, error rate, ROI) has never been built because the volume inputs he agreed to send on 14 Jan never arrived, so quantifying the cost of the 7 to 10 day cycle is the open move.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 Feb 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: '7 to 10 days... even if we are buying a single pen'
Avoma discovery call 24 Feb 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: 'users are not happy, the finance people are not happy, my boss is not happy'
Avoma call 14 Jan 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: transparency and cycle-time reduction as the goal

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with two people the calls name only by role, never by verified Salesforce name. On 14 Jan Perez said he would 'review it with a finance manager. She will give me the proper feedback if it fits in the budget', making her the budget gate, and he repeatedly deferred the go/no-go to his boss (the plant director) who set the pilot-first mandate and rejected the prior Ariba price. Neither the finance manager nor the boss has appeared on any call or carries a Salesforce contact role, so the economic buyer is mapped as a role but not engaged and not named. Getting Perez to name and introduce the finance manager and his boss is the required next step; no transcript spelling should be promoted to a named EB.
sources: Avoma call 14 Jan 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: 'I will review it with a finance manager. She will give me the proper feedback if it fits in the budget'
Avoma call 14 Jan 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: the boss said 'if it is the same as Ariba, it is a no go'
OpportunityContactRole: only Jesus Ochoa on record; no finance or executive contact captured

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are qualitative and buyer-stated: process transparency and visibility, cycle-time reduction from the current 7 to 10 days, and out-of-the-box handling of Mexican SAT tax compliance including the CFDI PDF plus XML validation and mandatory supplier contracts. On 24 Feb Devika confirmed those are solvable 'straight out of the box from Zycus, including the Mexico clearance and the CFDI validation'. Perez also insisted the AI/agent modules (ANA, Merlin) be in scope on 14 Jan 'because that is part of your differentiators', signalling differentiation criteria beyond core S2P. The criteria are not yet formalised: Perez committed to send written 'success criteria' his boss would sign off, and that document has not been delivered, so the criteria remain confirmed-directional but not documented.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 Feb 2026, Devika (Zycus): 'everything you have mentioned is solvable... straight out of the box from Zycus, including the Mexico clearance and the CFDI'
Avoma call 14 Jan 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: 'I want that included because it is part of your differentiators'
Avoma discovery call 24 Feb 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: commitment to send written success criteria

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, the process is a single-facility pilot at San Juan del Rio / Queretaro, chosen deliberately as Rassini's test lab ('this has been always the facility that works as a lab... if they make sense we scale up'), with success on ROI and user adoption as the gate to expand to the other eight facilities. The internal approval chain Perez mapped is heavy: requisitions pass through head of area, controlling, chief of finance, manager of finance and a dual-role commercial manager, and Perez wants the tool to re-engineer that. The evaluation process itself stalled when Jesus Ochoa left and Perez froze it pending a successor; the restart trigger is the new IT hire being onboarded, after which an onsite demonstration is the agreed next event.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 Feb 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: 'this has been always the facility that works as a lab... we scale up in the company'
Avoma call 7 May 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: 'we will put on hold this project until we find the successor of Jesus'
Salesforce Next_Step 7 May 2026: 'once he hires the project will restart and they would like us onsite for the demonstration'

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been touched: the deal is in Qualified with no pricing formally delivered, no MSA, Order Form or SOW discussed, and no procurement or InfoSec channel opened. What the calls do reveal is a compelling external forcing function, the Mexican SAT mandate requiring formal contracts for every supplier, which Perez called 'huge for us... otherwise it will be a nightmare', giving contract management a compliance-driven urgency once the project restarts. Visibility into the actual buying/legal paper path will only come after the pilot is scoped and pricing is on the table; today it is genuinely unknown.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 Feb 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: 'they are asking for contracts for every single supplier... that would be huge for us'
Salesforce stage: Qualified, forecast Pipeline; no pricing or contract activity logged

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed, specific and owner-named. Perez, mapping the process for his boss, found 'it is not that way. No one was happy. The users are not happy. The finance people are not happy. My boss is not happy', and that sourcing and procurement are run as one manual Excel/email process with no central purchasing department and 10 to 15 people buying. The 7 to 10 day approval cycle applies to every transaction regardless of value, suppliers get 'completely blindsided' on payment status, and the new SAT contract-per-supplier rule threatens 'a nightmare' without contract management. The pain owner is Perez as acting project lead escalating to his boss, whose reaction ('probably it is more important having the solution than we previously think') raised the project's internal priority.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 Feb 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: 'No one was happy. The users are not happy. The finance people are not happy. My boss is not happy'
Avoma discovery call 24 Feb 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: 7 to 10 day approval cycle on 'every single piece of transaction'
Avoma discovery call 24 Feb 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: SAT mandate 'contracts for every single supplier... would be huge for us'

champion.narrative: Jose Antonio Perez is a developing champion, committed but not yet driving. What has developed: he initiated the engagement (14 Jan), personally mapped Rassini's entire purchasing process for his boss, insisted the AI differentiator modules stay in scope, and phoned rather than emailed to hold the project when Jesus Ochoa left ('I don't want to just send you an email... I appreciate the call'), which signals genuine investment. What is still missing: he has not delivered the success criteria he committed to, has not multi-threaded Zycus to the finance manager or his boss, and has been passive through the eight-week freeze (Next Step 'no update' 29 Jun). He is now the single thread after Jesus's departure, so his engagement is both the deal's best asset and its biggest single point of failure.
sources: Avoma calls 14 Jan, 24 Feb and 7 May 2026: Perez led all three touchpoints
Avoma call 7 May 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: 'I don't want to just send you an email and just tell you, put it on hold'
Salesforce Next_Step 29 Jun 2026: 'no update'; no finance or executive stakeholder engaged

competition.narrative: There is no active rival in a bake-off; the live threat is inertia. SAP Ariba is a ghost from a prior evaluation that ended in sticker shock: on 14 Jan Perez relayed his boss saying 'if it is the same as Ariba, it is a no go', making Ariba the price benchmark rather than a competing bid. Karson positioned Zycus on transparent user-based pricing ('better or less expensive') and the Merlin/ANA agents as the differentiators Perez himself wanted in scope. With the project frozen on hiring and no success criteria delivered, the real competitor is the status quo, Rassini continuing to run its manual 7 to 10 day approval process and letting the pilot quietly lapse if the hire drags or budget priorities shift.
sources: Avoma call 14 Jan 2026, Jose Antonio Perez: 'if it is the same as Ariba, it is a no go'
Avoma call 14 Jan 2026, Karson (Zycus): pricing is 'user-based... very competitive, like better or less expensive'
Salesforce Next_Step 26 May and 29 Jun 2026: 'holding pattern until hiring' / 'no update'

deal_movement.summary: The deal has moved backward and then flat: a brief promotion to Formal Evaluation reverted to Qualified on 11 Jun, and every Next Step entry since 7 May records a holding pattern pending the IT hire. The last genuine two-way buyer exchange was the 7 May hold call, followed by an incoming 24 Jun email in which Perez declined to commit to Horizon.

items.change: Stage reverted from Formal Evaluation back to Qualified
Project placed on hold pending a successor for departed IT lead Jesus Ochoa
Perez declined a near-term Horizon commitment ('too early to confirm')
Division reclassified from Zycus to New Markets

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is actively bidding. SAP Ariba is a faded price ghost from a prior evaluation: Perez's boss said on 14 Jan that if Zycus matches the Ariba number 'it is a no go', so Ariba anchors price sensitivity but is not in a live bake-off (low, declined). The single strongest current threat is the status quo / do-nothing path, driven by the eight-week hiring freeze, undelivered success criteria and passive sponsorship, which risks the pilot being deprioritised or shelved (high). Zycus fit is strong (full S2P plus Merlin/ANA agents, transparent pricing, SAT/CFDI compliance out of the box) but untested because no demo or pricing has landed.

competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of the 7 to 10 day manual approval cycle and the SAT contract-compliance risk, and secure the onsite demo the moment the IT successor starts to re-ignite momentum before the pilot lapses

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus fits the buyer's stated functional and compliance criteria well; the one exposure is price against a self-funded small-facility budget scarred by prior Ariba sticker shock.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is early, single-threaded and frozen on a personnel gap; the win plan is to stay warm without chasing, arm Perez to justify the pilot internally, and widen the relationship so the deal survives another change.
flags: Stay warm, do not chase a demo now: keep a light value cadence and lock the restart trigger the moment Jesus's successor is hired.
Arm Perez with an ROI one-pager quantifying the 7 to 10 day approval cost and SAT contract-compliance risk to pre-justify the pilot budget to finance.
Multi-thread before restart: earn an intro to the finance manager and the plant-director boss so the deal is not lost with a single departure.

items.name: Jesus Ochoa
items.title: Information Technology Manager
items.risk: Loss of the technical lead froze the project; the successor hire is the blocker to restart.
items.sentiment: Departed 7 May 2026 for a pharma role; was technical lead and onsite demo point of contact, and his exit froze the project.

champion_strength.summary: Jose Antonio Perez is a developing champion: invested enough to hand-map the entire purchasing process and to phone rather than email when he had to pause the project, but he has not delivered the success criteria he committed to, has not brought the finance manager or his boss into the conversation, and has gone passive through the eight-week freeze. The deal is single-threaded on him and at risk if his focus shifts or the hire drags.
champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 14 Jan, 24 Feb, 7 May 2026; Salesforce Next_Step 29 Jun 2026 'no update'

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS reads 3/Negative and the calls corroborate a process-first buyer, but Perez personally asked to keep the ANA and Merlin agents in scope 'because that is part of your differentiators', so the AI story has a foothold with the champion even though the wider organisation is AI-neutral. Positioning is roughly right for the account, not materially under-sold.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 3 with AIS_Status Negative, consistent with three calls that stayed on classic S2P scoping, ERP timing and SAT compliance with no agentic/GenAI discussion. The one nuance the field understates: Perez wanted the ANA/Merlin agent modules retained as differentiators (14 Jan), so the champion is mildly curious even though the org appetite is resistant.

items.detail: IT lead Jesus Ochoa resigned 7 May with no successor; the project is frozen until a replacement is hired and onboarded.
items.source: Avoma call 7 May 2026, Jose Antonio Perez

items.detail: A $15-18M-revenue facility must self-fund the pilot, against prior Ariba sticker shock and a boss who said matching Ariba's price is a no-go.
items.source: Avoma call 14 Jan 2026, Jose Antonio Perez

items.detail: Deal is single-threaded on Perez; the budget-owning finance manager and the plant-director boss have never engaged directly.
items.source: Avoma calls 14 Jan and 24 Feb 2026; Salesforce contact roles

items.detail: The 1 Dec close date is not credible while the project is frozen on hiring with no restart date and no demo or pricing delivered.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: the last substantive call was 7 May and every touch since is a holding-pattern status note, with Perez declining a near-term commitment on 24 Jun.
items: Project on hold since 7 May pending IT hire (Avoma 7 May 2026).
Next Step 'holding pattern until hiring' 26 May and 'no update' 29 Jun 2026.
Perez declined a near-term Horizon commitment on 24 Jun 2026.

scope_change.from: Full source-to-pay suite rolled out across all nine Rassini facilities, including spend analytics (iAnalyze), big-bang
scope_change.to: Single small-facility pilot at San Juan del Rio / Queretaro, spend analytics dropped, phased source-to-contract first
scope_change.detail: On 14 Jan Perez's boss steered off a large launch to a pilot at a $15-18M-revenue lab facility, iAnalyze was removed from scope, and a phased source-to-contract-then-P2P path was floated over big-bang. This is a cost-defensive narrowing tied to prior Ariba sticker shock, aimed at proving ROI on a small self-funded footprint before scaling.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A single-facility S2P pilot frozen since 7 May when IT lead Jesus Ochoa quit; Jose Antonio Perez held the project rather than killing it, but with no successor hired, no success criteria delivered and no demo, the restart hinges entirely on Rassini's hiring.

win_position.text: Zycus has clean functional fit and a committed contact in Perez, but the deal has sat in Qualified since January and is now frozen on Rassini's hiring, so the position is early and fragile (Avoma 7 May; SF stage history).
No economic buyer is engaged: budget sits with an unnamed finance manager and Perez's boss, neither of whom has ever joined a call (Avoma 14 Jan, 24 Feb).
Scope narrowed from a nine-facility S2P rollout to a single small-facility pilot with spend analytics dropped, a cost-defensive signal tied to prior Ariba sticker shock (Avoma 14 Jan).

deal_momentum.text: Zero forward motion since the 7 May hold call; success criteria (committed 24 Feb) and volume inputs (committed 14 Jan) were never delivered (Avoma; Next Step 29 Jun 'no update').
Perez's 24 Jun reply declined the Horizon invite as 'too early to confirm', a status touch rather than project progress (Clari email 24 Jun).

customer_commitment.text: Perez proactively phoned to pause rather than drop the project and asked Zycus to come onsite once hiring completes (Avoma 7 May).
Commitment is single-threaded on Perez with no finance manager or executive sponsor engaged and no success criteria delivered in eight weeks (Avoma 24 Feb; Next Step 29 Jun).

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is inertia: with the IT lead gone and no hire, Rassini keeps running its manual 7 to 10 day approval process and the pilot could quietly lapse (Avoma 24 Feb, 7 May).
Price sensitivity is live: the boss said 'if it is the same as Ariba it is a no go' and the facility must self-fund a $15-18M-revenue pilot (Avoma 14 Jan).

deal_momentum.evidence: Eight weeks frozen since 7 May; no evaluation activity, committed inputs never delivered, Next Step 'no update' 29 Jun.

customer_commitment.evidence: Perez held the project and wants an onsite demo, but it is single-threaded with no EB engagement and no delivered success criteria.

deal_risk.evidence: Do-nothing inertia during the hiring freeze plus live price sensitivity against prior Ariba sticker shock on a self-funded facility.

critical_signals.text: The 7 May call was a hold call: Perez phoned to pause the project after IT lead Jesus Ochoa resigned, not to advance it.
Technical lead Jesus Ochoa left 7 May with no successor yet, leaving Jose Antonio Perez as the sole contact and the whole deal single-threaded.
No active rival: the only competitive shadow is a prior Ariba quote the boss rejected on price, so the real threat is do-nothing inertia during the hiring freeze.
Pricing must clear a $15-18M-revenue facility's self-funded budget and beat prior Ariba sticker shock, and no formal pricing has yet been delivered.

customer_preference.evidence: Perez was positive on Zycus differentiators and wanted ANA/Merlin in scope (14 Jan), but the project has been frozen since 7 May with no decisive lean or Zycus-specific next step requested.

business_case.evidence: Strong qualitative pain (7 to 10 day approval cycle, everyone unhappy, SAT contract mandate) but no ROI, savings or headcount figures quantified; volume inputs to build the case were never delivered.

items.expected_effect: Converts an open-ended freeze into a dated restart trigger so momentum resumes the moment the IT hire lands.

items.expected_effect: Speeds the new hire's onboarding into the evaluation and keeps Zycus front-of-mind through the gap.

items.expected_effect: Arms the champion to pre-justify the self-funded pilot budget to finance and blunts Ariba price comparisons.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — On 14 Jan Perez's boss steered off a large launch to a pilot at a $15-18M-revenue lab facility, iAnalyze was removed from scope, and a phased source-to-contract (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)

commentary.win_position: Win 6 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — On 14 Jan Perez's boss steered off a large launch to a pilot at a $15-18M-revenue lab facility, iAnalyze was….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (6), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 1 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
